best_agent_match.title: Public `depositFor` enables permissionless dusting that blocks victims from borrowing the dusted asset

findings.title: A reverting interest model on any synced asset can brick solvency-dependent flows for unrelated users

best_agent_match.title: A single reverting interest model on any synced asset can brick solvency checks for unrelated users

findings.title: Flash liquidation lets the liquidator set an arbitrary liquidation penalty by redepositing seized collateral

best_agent_match.title: No Validation That Liquidator Receives Sufficient Collateral

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Liquidation Allows Theft of Collateral Without Full Repayment
rejected_candidates.reason: As written, this is incorrect: if the callback does not restore solvency, the whole transaction reverts and the collateral transfer is not finalized. The real issue is the solvency-only post-check that allows excess collateral extraction via redepositing seized collateral, captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation Callback Enables Reentrancy Attack
rejected_candidates.reason: The generic reentrancy claim is too broad and unsupported. The concrete reportable problem is the callback's ability to call `depositFor` and satisfy only the final solvency check, captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value for Token Transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: `SafeERC20.safeTransfer` and `safeTransferFrom` already handle tokens that return `false` or no value; this is a misunderstanding of the library behavior.

rejected_candidates.title: LTV Can Be Set to 100% Leaving No Liquidation Buffer
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a governance/risk-parameter choice, not a code vulnerability in the reviewed contracts.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation Does Not Validate Repayment Success
rejected_candidates.reason: The callback does not need to repay the advertised full amount, but the raw candidate's reasoning is imprecise. The concrete exploit is the solvency-only post-check combined with public `depositFor`, captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Entry Fee Calculation May Result in Zero Fees
rejected_candidates.reason: Integer truncation on tiny amounts only waives dust-level fees and does not create meaningful protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Interest Accrual Can Be Blocked by Paused Silo for All Users
rejected_candidates.reason: Halting user actions while a silo is paused is expected emergency-pause behavior, not a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation That Liquidator Receives Sufficient Collateral
rejected_candidates.reason: This is liquidator/oracle economics, not a protocol bug; the contract does not promise liquidation profitability.
